codeReview.summary: The implementation correctly adds a DB-aware health endpoint, sets up specific CORS origins, configures Scalar API documentation, and provides full e2e coverage for these elements. Code perfectly aligns with requirements.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker was assigned the api-domain-worker skill, which heavily prescribes a DDD implementation process (entities, use-cases, repositories, domain unit tests). The worker correctly recognized this was just a framework wiring task (health controller, CORS, Swagger) and skipped the domain steps, leading to a procedure deviation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows worker explicitly loading api-domain-worker skill, then building only a NestJS controller and module. handoff.skillFeedback.followedProcedure is false.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Skill demands writing tests first (TDD), but worker deviated because bootstrap wiring (like setting up app.enableCors and Swagger in main.ts) is difficult to test before the basic NestJS bootstrapping is written.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff.skillFeedback.deviations states 'Implemented the health/CORS/docs wiring first... required minimal scaffolding before meaningful endpoint tests could be executed.'

summary: Review passed. The worker correctly implemented the framework-level health and CORS configurations despite being given a domain-centric skill.
